_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised autopilot, LOCAL track)
_meta.note: FL locals batch — Tallahassee Mayor (Dailey) + Tallahassee City Commission Seat 1 (Porter — SEAT CORRECTION from stale Seat 5) + Seat 3 (Matlow, mayoral candidate) + Tampa City Council District 1 (Clendenin, Council Chair) + Tampa City Council District 4 (Carlson, 2027 mayoral candidate). Roster verified June 2026. Tallahassee 287(g) vote cleanly splits 3-2: Dailey/Richardson/Williams-Cox vote to MAINTAIN ICE cooperation; Matlow/Porter move to rescind. Same 3-2 split on millage above rollback. Dailey is a documented professed Christian (Faith Presbyterian) and pro-police Dem — flagged as high-value deviations. Reset_unspecified true; FALSE only where documented; null where no evidence.

set.office: City Commissioner, Seat 1, City of Tallahassee, Florida (SEAT CORRECTION: prior record listed Seat 5; talgov.com + WCTV + Ballotpedia confirm Seat 1 — Williams-Cox holds Seat 5; first elected 2020 at age 27, youngest woman ever elected in Tallahassee; re-elected outright Aug 20, 2024 nonpartisan primary with ~53%; current term to Nov 2028)

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of current faith practice — raised in pro-life religious household but has explicitly broken from that family tradition; no church affiliation appears in campaign or official bio
profile.education: MPA Public Administration & Policy, Florida State University; MS Urban & Regional Planning, FSU; BA, FSU
profile.background: City Commissioner, Seat 1, City of Tallahassee, FL — first elected 2020 at age 27 (youngest woman ever elected in Tallahassee); re-elected outright Aug 20, 2024 nonpartisan primary with ~53%; current term to Nov 2028. Born and raised in São Paulo, Brazil; moved to South Florida and then Tallahassee for college (three FSU degrees). Pre-commission: Office of Criminal Conflict and Civil Regional Counsel (First Region); Florida Gubernatorial Fellow at the Florida Dept of Environmental Protection; Tallahassee/Leon Commission on the Status of Women & Girls (2016-2019); Providence Neighborhood Association. Organizing member of the Florida chapter of Local Progress (national progressive municipal-officials network).

jacqueline-jack-porter.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] SEAT CORRECTION: stale DB record listed Seat 5; verified Seat 1 per talgov.com, WCTV, and Ballotpedia (Williams-Cox holds Seat 5). Roster verified: re-elected outright Aug 2024 with ~53%; current term to Nov 2028. HARD evidence: voted YES with Matlow + Dailey on the Oct 2021 abortion-access resolution (3-2; Richardson + Williams-Cox dissented) — sanctity_of_life q0/q1 FALSE (WCTV, Tallahassee Reports). Endorsed by Equality Florida 2024 (Tallahassee Reports), aligned with Capital Equality Democratic Caucus — biblical_marriage q0/q2/q3 FALSE. Voted with Matlow Oct 22, 2025 to RESCIND Tallahassee's 287(g) ICE agreement (motion failed 2-3, with Dailey/Richardson/Williams-Cox voting to keep) — border_immigration q0/q1/q2 all FALSE (WFSU, Tallahassee Reports); said she was 'truly ashamed of our city right now and our complicity in our president and governor's unconstitutional, immoral and dangerous agendas.' SOLE NO vote (4-1) in Nov 2025 against a $625K federal grant to hire 5 community policing officers, stating hiring more officers 'did not impact crime' — public_justice q0/q3 FALSE (Tallahassee Reports). DEVIATION: consistent NO vote on millage above rollback (2023, 2024, 2025) — on paper this is conservative fiscal restraint (econ q2 TRUE) but motivation is explicitly to reduce police line items, not Reagan fiscal conservatism. Local Progress affiliation pushes CRT-aligned policy frameworks — family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE (Tallahassee Reports). No public Christian faith profession found.

0.note: Voted YES (3-2) on Oct 2021 Tallahassee resolution affirming access to abortion as 'a public health issue' and 'a legal and safe procedure.'

1.note: YES vote on the same Oct 2021 city resolution directing municipal advocacy for abortion access — affirmative pro-public-funding posture.

0.note: Endorsed by Equality Florida 2024 (state's largest LGBTQ civil-rights org, screens for same-sex marriage support).

2.note: Equality Florida endorsement; aligned with Capital Equality Democratic Caucus campaign infrastructure.

3.note: Equality Florida endorsement; that organization actively opposes Florida's gender-affirming-care restrictions for minors.

2.note: Organizing member of Local Progress, the national progressive municipal-officials network whose policy library actively promotes CRT-aligned frameworks; documented use of city resources for that affiliation.

Consistent NO vote on millage rates above rollback (2023 with Matlow on 0.62-mill increase; 2024 with Matlow seconding motion to return to rolled-back rate; 2025 forced commission to lower from 4.42 to 4.4072). DEVIATION CAVEAT: stated motivation is opposition to police funding, not general fiscal conservatism — recorded vote nonetheless aligns with rubric q2.

0.note: Sole NO vote (4-1) Nov 2025 against $625K federal community-policing grant to hire 5 new TPD officers; stated hiring more officers 'did not impact crime.'

3.note: Stated 'I don't believe the police budget, or the police are equipped to handle [crime] in the best way' — said alternatives should be 'investing in our housing, investing in our social services, investing in mental health services.' Affiliated with Local Progress, which advocates law-enforcement budget reductions.

0.note: Public statement: 'I'm truly ashamed of our city right now and our complicity in our president and governor's unconstitutional, immoral and dangerous agendas' on immigration enforcement.

1.note: Voted Oct 22, 2025 to RESCIND Tallahassee's 287(g) agreement (motion failed 2-3); seconded Matlow's motion.

2.note: Anti-287(g) cooperation vote — moved to terminate the city's federal ICE-cooperation agreement.

set.office: City Commissioner, Seat 3, City of Tallahassee, Florida (assumed office Nov 19, 2018; current Seat 3 term ends Nov 16, 2026; NOT seeking re-election to Seat 3 — filed Aug 11, 2025 to run for Tallahassee Mayor in the Aug 18, 2026 primary)

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND — no church affiliation, faith profession, or religious identification in campaign bio, official bio, news coverage, or social
profile.education: NO EVIDENCE FOUND — campaign bio, Ballotpedia, and LinkedIn fragments do not list a college/university; he attended schools in Tallahassee
profile.background: City Commissioner, Seat 3, City of Tallahassee, FL — assumed office Nov 19, 2018; current term to Nov 16, 2026. Filed Aug 11, 2025 for Tallahassee Mayor (Aug 18, 2026 primary) — Seat 3 will be open. Co-founded Gaines Street Pies (with Midtown Pies, Northside Pies + Warhorse Whiskey Bar / The Wilbury); ~85 employees. Grew up on Tallahassee's southside, raised in poverty by single mother of four. Married to Sarah; two sons. Pre-commission: president of Gaines Street Community Association, Municipal Code Enforcement Board. Leading voice of the 2-member progressive minority bloc (with Porter); endorsed Bernie Sanders for President 2020. Runs the One Tallahassee PAC.

jeremy-matlow.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Seat 3, sworn Nov 19, 2018; term ends Nov 16, 2026; NOT seeking re-election to Seat 3 — filed Aug 11, 2025 to run for Tallahassee Mayor in the Aug 18, 2026 primary (Seat 3 will be open). Leading progressive bloc voice (with Porter); endorsed Bernie Sanders 2020. HARD evidence: PERSONALLY MOVED the Oct 2021 abortion-access resolution (passed 3-2; Matlow + Porter + Dailey YES); after Dobbs said the work 'is more important than ever' — sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE (Florida Politics, WCTV, Tallahassee Reports). Equality Florida 'Friend' status 2026 Tallahassee Gala; aligned with Capital Equality Democratic Caucus — biblical_marriage q2/q4 FALSE (Equality FL). May 2026 publicly defended Pride Festival visibility against SB 1134 ('outright cruel,' attacked law for blocking posts about 'Pride Festival') — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE; family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE (WCTV DEI coverage). MADE motion to RESCIND Tallahassee's 287(g) ICE agreement Oct 22, 2025 (failed 2-3, Matlow + Porter YES; Dailey/Richardson/Williams-Cox NO) — border_immigration q0/q1/q2 all FALSE (WTXL, WFSU). Said he was 'disgusted' by ICE raids. PRE-MOTION led Aug 20, 2025 procedural vote to direct city attorney to explore challenging 287(g) in court. Feb 2023 publicly called FL constitutional-carry legislation 'one of the most reckless proposals' — self_defense q0 FALSE. Voted against multiple TPD-funding budget cycles; PAC (One Tallahassee) amplified video described as 'anti-law enforcement' promoting Inman-Johnson — public_justice q0/q3 FALSE (Tallahassee Reports). DEVIATION: Consistent NO on millage above rollback (2023 8.5% hike, 2024 4.42 millage, 2025 forced down from 4.42 to 4.4072) — econ q2 TRUE on paper, but explicitly motivated by anti-fire-fee / anti-establishment progressive populism, not Reagan fiscal conservatism. NO public Christian faith profession found in any source.

0.note: Personally moved the Oct 2021 Tallahassee abortion-access resolution urging the legislature to protect 'a woman's right to choose' (passed 3-2). Post-Dobbs said the work 'is more important than ever'; attacked his 2024 opponent's 'extreme anti-choice' donations.

2.note: Publicly attacked FL SB 1134 (anti-DEI law) for blocking commissioners' posts about 'a Pride Festival'; Equality Florida 'Friend' designation 2026 Tallahassee Gala.

4.note: Publicly defended Pride Festival visibility against state restrictions in May 2026 DEI discussion; campaigned at Tallahassee Pridefest as a mayoral candidate.

2.note: Opposed FL anti-DEI law SB 1134, calling its impacts 'outright cruel' and saying 'anything that gives Black people money' would be blocked.

Consistent NO on millage above rollback: FY2024 8.5% property-tax increase, FY2025 4.42 millage, FY2026 forced down from 4.42 to 4.4072. Argued city should 'operate within our means.' DEVIATION CAVEAT: motivation is anti-fire-fee / anti-establishment progressive populism, not conservative fiscal restraint.

0.note: Voted against multiple TPD-funding budget cycles; One Tallahassee PAC amplified video described as 'anti-law enforcement' promoting Inman-Johnson who attacked newly sworn TPD officers as 'paid stooges.'

3.note: Anti-TPD-tax votes + PAC amplification of anti-law-enforcement content. Not a literal defund vote but rubric posture fails.

0.note: Said he was 'disgusted' by ICE raids and that they 'don't reflect the values of the community.'

1.note: Moved Aug 20, 2025 to have city attorney explore challenging 287(g) in court; then made the Oct 22, 2025 motion to RESCIND 287(g) entirely (failed 2-3).

2.note: MADE the motion to rescind Tallahassee's 287(g) ICE agreement Oct 22, 2025; motion failed 2-3 (Matlow + Porter YES to rescind; Dailey/Richardson/Williams-Cox NO).

0.note: Feb 2023 publicly called Florida's constitutional-carry legislation 'one of the most reckless proposals.'

set.office: Mayor, City of Tallahassee, Florida (127th Mayor; first elected Nov 6, 2018 with 51.4% in runoff vs. Dustin Daniels; re-elected Nov 8, 2022 with 53.1% in runoff vs. Kristin Dozier; current term ends Nov 16, 2026; announced Aug 11, 2025 he will NOT seek a third term — open-seat 2026 race)

profile.religion: Professed Christian; attends Faith Presbyterian Church in Tallahassee; publicly participated in citywide day of fasting and prayer for Tallahassee in March 2021
profile.education: BA Political Science, Florida State University; MPA, Reubin O'D. Askew School of Public Administration & Policy (FSU); MA Urban and Regional Planning, London School of Economics
profile.background: 127th Mayor of Tallahassee, Democrat. Born Dec 7, 1972 in Miami. Served three terms on Leon County Commission District 3 (2006-2018), chairing 2010-11 and 2016-17. Elected Mayor Nov 6, 2018 (succeeded Andrew Gillum); re-elected 2022. Announced Aug 11, 2025 he will not seek a third term; 2026 mayoral race is open-seat. Wife Virginia; two sons. Father (J. Scott Dailey) was a Leon County School Board member. Sigma Chi at FSU.

john-e-dailey.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: 127th Mayor of Tallahassee, re-elected 2022, term to Nov 16, 2026; announced Aug 11, 2025 he will NOT seek a third term — 2026 race is open-seat (Wikipedia, WCTV). HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION #1: Dailey is a professed Christian who attends Faith Presbyterian Church in Tallahassee; publicly participated in the citywide day of fasting and prayer for Tallahassee in March 2021 — christian_liberty q0 TRUE (FloridaCapitalTea, WCTV). HARD evidence on cultural cells: voted YES (3-2) on Oct 2021 abortion-access resolution with Matlow + Porter (Richardson + Williams-Cox dissented) — sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE (WCTV, Tallahassee Reports). Issued annual June Pride Month proclamations as Mayor ('hate has no place here'); endorsed by Equality Florida — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (WTXL). Signed July 2023 GLSEN Rise Up pledge backing LGBTQ youth advocacy in the face of state laws restricting gender-identity content — biblical_marriage q3 FALSE (Tallahassee Reports). HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION #2: voted (3-2 with Williams-Cox and Richardson) AGAINST rescinding TPD's 287(g) ICE agreement Oct 22-23, 2025; said city must follow legal opinion and remain 'in full compliance' with state/federal law — border_immigration q1 TRUE, q2 TRUE (WFSU, Tallahassee Reports). HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION #3: AFFIRMATIVE pro-police record — Big Bend PBA publicly stated 'Dailey never cut police funding' (2022 mailer rebuttal of his Dem challenger Dozier); consistently voted to increase TPD staffing and salaries; FY2024 budget specifically funded 20 new TPD officers; voted Jan 15, 2025 to repeal the Citizens Police Review Board after state HB 601 limited oversight authority — public_justice q0 TRUE, q3 TRUE (Tallahassee Reports, WTXL). ECON: deciding YES vote (3-2) Sept 27, 2023 for $1.12B FY2024 budget with 4.45 millage = 8.5% above rollback; voted YES again Sept 2024 on FY2025 budget at 4.42 millage above rollback — economic_stewardship q2 FALSE (WFSU, Tallahassee Reports). HOME RULE: leftward defiance of FL preemption on HB1 anti-protest bill ('overreach and disregard of home rule'); under conservative-rubric reading this is null on refuse_state_overreach q0. NO direct city-level evidence on schools (mayor lane), election administration, christian-liberty q1-q2, public_justice q1-q2, or self-defense (FL preemption) — those cells null. Endorsed by Equality Florida but opposed by Ruth's List (which backed his 2022 Dem challenger Dozier with a 'misogynistic' attack frame) — confirms his right-flank-Dem positioning.

0.note: Voted YES (3-2) Oct 27, 2021 with Matlow + Porter on Tallahassee resolution urging FL Legislature to protect access to reproductive healthcare, including abortion.

3.note: Joined July 2023 GLSEN Rise Up pledge backing LGBTQ youth advocacy in the face of state laws restricting gender-identity content.

4.note: Issued annual June Pride Month proclamations as Mayor ('hate has no place here'); also issued LGBTQ+ History Month proclamation; endorsed by Equality Florida.

0.note: Identified as a 'professed Christian' who attends Faith Presbyterian Church in Tallahassee; publicly participated in citywide day of fasting and prayer for Tallahassee in March 2021.

2.note: Deciding YES vote (3-2) Sept 27, 2023 for FY2024 $1.12B budget at 4.45 millage = 8.5% increase above rollback; YES again Sept 2024 on FY2025 budget at 4.42 millage (rollback would have required ~$3.9M in cuts).

0.note: Big Bend PBA publicly stated 'Dailey never cut police funding'; FY2024 budget specifically funded 20 new TPD officers + salary increases; voted Jan 15, 2025 to repeal CPRB after state law HB 601 limited oversight authority.

3.note: Police union publicly defended his record; consistently voted for budgets growing TPD funding (FY2019-20 $59M; FY22-23 $66.9M; FY24 added 20 officers + salary increases).

1.note: Voted Oct 22, 2025 (3-2) to MAINTAIN Tallahassee's 287(g) ICE agreement against the Matlow/Porter motion to rescind; said city must follow legal opinion and remain 'in full compliance' with state/federal law. Tallahassee is functionally not a sanctuary jurisdiction.

2.note: Affirmatively voted to keep TPD's 287(g) federal ICE-cooperation agreement intact (Oct 2025, 3-2).

set.office: City Council, District 1 (At-Large), City of Tampa, Florida (sworn May 1, 2023 after April 25, 2023 runoff vs. Sonja Brookins 64.51%; current term ends May 1, 2027; currently serves as Council Chair — first elected May 2025, re-elected 2026)

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND — no church affiliation, faith profession, or invocation activity in any publicly available source
profile.education: Public schools (central Florida); Valencia Community College; University of Colorado, Boulder
profile.background: City Council, District 1 (At-Large), City of Tampa, FL — Tampa's first openly LGBTQ+ council member; sworn May 1, 2023; currently serves as Council Chair (first elected May 2025, re-elected 2026); term ends May 2027. Native of Sanford, FL; Tampa resident since ~1985; partner of 35+ years (raised son and daughter together, all attended Tampa public schools). Retired FAA air traffic controller (32 years). Long-time Florida Democratic Party operative: first openly gay DNC committee member from Florida (elected 2008), First Vice Chair of Florida Democratic Party (elected 2013). 1994 lobbied Air Traffic Controllers Union to extend benefits to same-sex couples. Began activism in 1975 with Jimmy Carter. Named one of Watermark's 10 Most Remarkable People of 2023. As Council Chair he is in line of succession if Mayor Castor were removed under FL AG's March 2026 sanctuary-removal threat.

alan-clendenin.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 1 (At-Large) Tampa City Council, sworn May 1, 2023, term to May 2027; currently serves as Council Chair (Florida Politics — re-elected Chair 2026 'signaling stability after last year's fiasco'). Tampa's first openly LGBTQ+ council member; long-time FL Democratic Party operative (DNC committee member, First VP). HARD evidence: openly gay, partner of 35+ years (raised two children together); 1994 lobbied union to extend benefits to same-sex couples; endorsed by Equality Florida Action PAC and LGBTQ Victory Fund — biblical_marriage q0 FALSE, q2 FALSE (Watermark, Victory Fund). As Council Chair personally spoke at Tampa's June 2026 Pride flag-raising at Old City Hall, 'highlighting Tampa's commitment to inclusion, the Stonewall Riots and the nine-year mark of Pulse' — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (tampa.gov, Watermark). ECON: Sept 2023 was one of three council members who voted IN FAVOR of Mayor Castor's millage hike from 6.21 to 7.21 (16% property tax increase); called the rejection 'dead on arrival' but supported the increase ('These are needs, they're not wants') — economic_stewardship q2 FALSE (Fox 13, WUSF). HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION: AFFIRMATIVE pro-police stance — 'Our crime's the lowest it's been in decades. This is not accidental, because we've had the investment in crime prevention in our police and our first responders'; campaign position: 'properly funding police and first responders and encouraging their presence in all neighborhoods will be extremely helpful in reducing the crime rate' — public_justice q0 TRUE, q3 TRUE (Creative Loafing Tampa). BORDER: publicly criticized TPD's 2026 revision toward ICE-compliance as 'appeasing the Attorney General' rather than substantive law-enforcement policy — border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE (WMNF). NO documented direct positions on abortion (despite long Dem leadership, no Tampa-level vote located — q0/q1 null per rubric anti-inference rule), schools (city lane), election administration, christian-liberty cells (no faith profession surfaced), self-defense, or refuse-state-overreach in conservative-rubric sense — those cells null. As Council Chair he is in line of succession if Mayor Castor were removed under the FL AG's March 2026 sanctuary-removal threat; he has publicly sided with Castor against the AG.

0.note: Openly gay; 35+ year partner; raised son and daughter together; in 1994 lobbied Air Traffic Controllers Union to extend benefits to same-sex couples; Tampa's first openly LGBTQ+ council member.

2.note: Endorsed by and aligned with Equality Florida Action PAC and LGBTQ+ Victory Fund; active LGBTQ+ advocate since 1994 union-benefits campaign.

4.note: As Council Chair, personally spoke at Tampa's June 2026 Pride flag-raising at Old City Hall, 'highlighting Tampa's commitment to inclusion, the Stonewall Riots and the nine-year mark of Pulse.'

2.note: Sept 2023, one of three voting IN FAVOR of Mayor Castor's millage hike from 6.21 to 7.21 (16% above-rollback property tax increase); said 'These are needs, they're not wants.'

0.note: Affirmative public statement: 'Our crime's the lowest it's been in decades. This is not accidental, because we've had the investment in crime prevention in our police and our first responders.' Cites $380M property tax revenue as direct support for police and fire.

3.note: Campaign position: 'properly funding police and first responders and encouraging their presence in all neighborhoods will be extremely helpful in reducing the crime rate' — no defund posture identified anywhere.

1.note: Publicly criticized Tampa PD's 2026 revision toward ICE-compliance as 'appeasing the Attorney General' rather than substantive law-enforcement policy; framed federal/state compliance push negatively.

2.note: Characterized Tampa's tightened cooperation as appeasement, signaling opposition to enforcement-side expansion (TPD signed 287(g) under state pressure; Clendenin's framing opposes the policy shift).

set.office: City Council, District 4 (South Tampa), City of Tampa, Florida (elected March 2019, sworn May 1, 2019; re-elected March 7, 2023 vs. Blake Casper 59.3-40.7%; current term ends May 1, 2027; filed April 2026 for the 2027 Tampa Mayor race — not seeking District 4 re-election)

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of specific church/denomination; only public religion-coded statement was an ecumenical 'whatever your religion is, please say a prayer' appeal during Hurricane Milton (CNN, Oct 2024) — explicitly pluralist, not a Christian profession
profile.education: BA Marketing, University of Tampa; MPA, Harvard Kennedy School of Government; MBA studies, National University of Singapore (Rotary International Scholar); Harvard Law School Negotiations Program; Leadership Florida Class XXIII
profile.background: Tampa City Council, District 4 (South Tampa), 2-term Dem with watchdog/transparency reputation; sworn May 1, 2019, re-elected March 2023, term to May 2027. Filed April 2026 for the 2027 Tampa mayoral race. President of Tucker/Hall (Tampa public-affairs/PR firm); 30+ years advising Fortune 500 corporations and global organizations. International President of PROI Worldwide (largest network of PR firms) 2007-2009. Senior marketing consultant in Singapore 1989-1994 as Rotary International Scholar. Co-founded Tampa Arts Alliance, Cafe Con Tampa. Board service: Florida Chamber Foundation, Florida TaxWatch, Florida Museum of History, Florida House DC. Father of three sons. The council's most vocal Castor critic — branded 'fiscal conservative-leaning Dem.' Late 2024 attempted to nominate Tampa Bay Young Republicans director Jake Hoffman to a city advisory board (drew loud LGBTQ pushback).

bill-carlson.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Tampa City Council District 4 (South Tampa), 2-term; current term to May 2027; FILED April 2026 for the 2027 Tampa mayoral race (not seeking District 4 re-election). Council races formally nonpartisan; Carlson is a registered Democrat with watchdog/transparency reputation — the council's loudest Castor critic. HARD evidence: voted YES (5-2 majority) on Tampa's June 2022 reproductive-rights resolution; criticized it as too weak and tried to move it to ordinance form to materially shield abortion access from state restrictions — sanctity_of_life q0/q1 FALSE (Creative Loafing Tampa, WTSP). DEVIATION/POSITIVE for rubric: voted NO on Mayor Castor's FY2024 millage increase from 6.21 → 7.21 (16% hike), arguing 'find the funding somewhere else'; explicitly proposed the rollback rate (5.8) as one of 4 votes defeating the hike 4-3; stated 'I am philosophically against a tax increase' — economic_stewardship q1 TRUE, q2 TRUE (WTSP, WMNF, WUSF). LGBTQ: publicly affirmed the LGBTQ community during Pride 2023 ('scary time' for them); rejected being labeled 'anti-LGBTQ' ('anybody that knows me would think that's ridiculous'); did not oppose Tampa's annual Pride flag at Old City Hall — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (Florida Politics, tampa.gov). POLICE: TPD union (Tampa PBA) declined to endorse his 2023 re-election bid, backing his challenger — PBA president stated Carlson 'often said he supported law enforcement but sometimes voted in ways that contradicted that support'; named Public Safety Committee Chair then removed within 24 hours after law-enforcement pushback; pushed Citizen Review Board subpoena power; later sponsored (then withdrew) proposal to dissolve the CRB calling it 'toothless' — public_justice cells MIXED, no clear TRUE on affirmative-partnership cell (Florida Politics, Creative Loafing). CHRISTIAN LIBERTY: only religion-coded statement is the ecumenical 'whatever your religion is, please say a prayer' appeal during Hurricane Milton (CNN, Oct 2024) — explicitly pluralist, not a Christian profession — christian_liberty q0 null. NO direct on-record vote or statement on Tampa 287(g) (City Council 'have yet to publicly comment'), election administration, family/school cells (city lane), constitutional carry, or red-flag/mag bans — those cells null. Hoffman / 'Trumpette' appointment episodes suggest he is courting fiscal-conservative allies for 2027 mayoral run.

0.note: Voted YES (5-2 majority) on Tampa's June 2022 reproductive-rights resolution; criticized it as too weak and tried to move it to ordinance form so the city could materially shield abortion access from state restrictions.

1.note: Pushed for stronger ordinance language directing city resources to shield abortion access — affirmative pro-public-funding posture.

4.note: Publicly affirmed LGBTQ community during 2023 Pride Month, called it a 'scary time' for them; rejected being labeled 'anti-LGBTQ'; did not oppose Tampa's annual Pride flag-raising at Old City Hall.

1.note: Voted NO on Mayor Castor's FY2024 millage increase from 6.21 → 7.21 (16% property tax hike), arguing the city should 'find the funding somewhere else' rather than burden residents.

2.note: Explicitly proposed the rollback rate (5.8); one of 4 votes defeating Castor's millage increase 4-3; stated 'I am philosophically against a tax increase.'
